title: Rei and the Left Handed Path
display_title: Rei and the Left Handed Path
wikitext:
[]: Rei and the Left Handed Path             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Thu Oct 22, 2015 7:02 pm I think everything about Rei represents the occult (I know, I know, it's all "window dressing," but bear with me). First, some etymology. The word occult, comes from the Latin "occultus" or "hidden". Now, consider Rei. At the the very beginning of the series, the fist we know of her is her voice as she is veiled by a screen that reads "SOUND ONLY." Then, there are things that are only very slowly revealed throughout the series...and the true nature of Rei is literally hidden in the dark depths below Nerv and Tokyo 3. But it's a matter of (symbolically speaking) of light and darkness, upright and inversion. To that end, look at the city in its "modes." On the surface, the city stands normally and kind of glows, then it gets inverted when lowers into the ground and becomes darkened. Thus, we have a kind of yin-tang thing going on. Now, Rei herself and Lilith by extension is the yin to Kaworu/Adam's yang (and to some extent Asuka in variance of this dynamic regarding "femininity"). Now, in occultism, the moon is often associated with feminine aspects (as in pagan earth mother stuff) and so, one could relate Rei as being symbolic to spiritual (as in pagan, polytheistic, “magical”) aspects, especially since her own namesake roughly translates to "ghost". I think this is part of the reason why she's barely visible in the group pic in EoE, in that sense, she kind of represents a shadow...Even her physical form is a kind of "reflection" of someone (Yui) who isn't there anymore. To that extent, if the angels (and by extension Kaworu/Adam) represent upright crosses representing then, Rei/Lilith is symbolized by the variant of occultism represented by the inverted cross or pentagram. (This is further evidenced by Lilith being on the lower end of the "cross of Lorraine" flashback where Lilith and Unit 01 were still connected.) Also, Lilith resided in the GeoFront which was buried (i.e. hidden) in darkness which only furthers this motif. Now, whether this is indicative of her being inherently "satanic" is debatable, but she is supposed to represent (according the myth) the first rejected wife of Adam. This "rejection" fills the much greater role of the "rejected stone, i.e. the blacked stone/lead" of the alchemical precepts involved in the series. Anyway, if NGE were a fantasy tale of some kind, Rei would be depicted as the all-black wearing evil witch (or evil witch in training depending how one might view the Akagis and Yui within that scenario). Certainly Lilith would fit this mold, what with being the face of the Apocalypse and all (granted, that was actually GNR/K a separate being from both Rei/Lilith and Kaworu/Adam, as the true “18th angel/new FAR). So, with this in mind, Rei being a child of shadows, she can’t exist in “light” or in a “divine” state and trying to do so causes abnormalities or stangeness. Just look at what happens when the last of her “occultus”-ness vanishes (the reveal of the Reiquarium) she literally starts the process of falling apart. This is because there is now more mystery to her after that point she’s been “revealed” thus the smoke and mirror illusion begins to dissipate like mirage. Furthermore, trying to exalt this “dark entity” to a god(dess)-like status is a problem here as she represents the perpetual shadow who wants to be inverted (hell even Lilith as a “tree of life” is an inversion with the branches going downward…but maybe it’s a double inversion since she represents man (tree of wisdom) and should’ve been upright from the beginning…) Nevertheless, Lilith wants to have her head tilting back (the way she looks when GNR is dying) because that’s her natural state. Thus, there are perpetual aspects that will always remain mysterious because answering them will on further unravel her mystique and destroy her inherent nature (the most egregious being her “bookend” appearances).

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 23, 2015 3:00 am Although he obliterated many of his posts on the way out, you might find any surviving Rei-oriented posts of Zuggy 's interesting, as this occult Rei notion was one of his obsessions.

[]: Re: Rei and the Left Handed Path             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Oct 23, 2015 2:17 pm SawItAtAge10 wrote: Nevertheless, Lilith wants to have her head tilting back (the way she looks when GNR is dying) because that’s her natural state.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Oct 28, 2015 8:14 pm Posting just to say thank you OP, some really interesting stuff here. And thanks to the other members for the links, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Sat Nov 07, 2015 11:16 am Dream wrote: Posting just to say thank you OP, some really interesting stuff here. And thanks to the other members for the links, as well.
